content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 11:21 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct!

Please write an integration guide for Orion API v2.3.0 in Markdown format and save it to the day21/ directory. Include the authentication flow, usage examples for key endpoints, and error handling recommendations.

arguments.content: # Orion API v2.3.0 Integration Guide **Version:** v2.3.0 **Last Updated:** April 13, 2026 **Author:** Alex Zhang, Backend Tech Lead **Contact:** alex.zhang@oriontech.com --- ## Table of Contents 1. [Overview](#overview) 2. [Quick Start](#quick-start) 3. [Authentication](#authentication) 4. [Core Endpoints](#core-endpoints) 5. [Error Handling](#error-handling) 6. [Rate Limiting](#rate-limiting) 7. [Webhooks](#webhooks) 8. [SDKs & Tools](#sdks--tools) --- ## Overview Orion API v2.3.0 provides RESTful access to Project Orion's project management capabilities, including real-time notifications, advanced search, and role-based access control. ### Base URL ``` Production: https://api.oriontech.com/v2 Sandbox: https://api-sandbox.oriontech.com/v2 ``` ### Content Type All requests should include: ```http Content-Type: application/json Accept: application/json ``` --- ## Quick Start ### 1. Get API Credentials Register your application in the Orion Developer Portal to obtain: - `client_id`: Your application identifier - `client_secret`: Your application secret (keep secure) ### 2. Authenticate ```bash curl -X POST https://api.oriontech.com/v2/auth/token \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "grant_type": "client_credentials", "client_id": "YOUR_CLIENT_ID", "client_secret": "YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET" }' ``` ### 3. Make Your First Request ```bash curl -X GET https://api.oriontech.com/v2/projects \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" ``` --- ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials Flow Orion API uses OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials for machine-to-machine authentication. #### Step 1: Request Access Token **Endpoint:** `POST /auth/token` **Request:** ```json { "grant_type": "client_credentials", "client_id": "your_client_id", "client_secret": "your_client_secret", "scope": "projects:read tasks:write" } ``` **Response:** ```json { "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...", "token_type": "Bearer", "expires_in": 3600, "scope": "projects:read tasks:write" } ``` #### Step 2: Use Access Token Include the token in all API requests: ```http Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN ``` #### Step 3: Token Refresh Tokens expire after 3600 seconds (1 hour). Request a new token before expiration. ### JWT Token Structure Orion API tokens are JWTs containing: ```json { "sub": "user_uuid", "org": "organization_uuid", "roles": ["admin", "editor"], "permissions": ["project:read", "task:write"], "iat": 1712970000, "exp": 1712973600 } ``` ### Authorization Scopes | Scope | Description | |-------|-------------| | `projects:read` | Read project data | | `projects:write` | Create and modify projects | | `tasks:read` | Read task data | | `tasks:write` | Create and modify tasks | | `notifications:read` | Read notifications | | `search:execute` | Execute search queries | | `admin` | Administrative operations | --- ## Core Endpoints ### Projects #### List Projects ```http GET /projects?page=1&per_page=20 ``` **Response:** ```json { "data": [ { "id": "proj_abc123", "name": "Q2 Marketing Campaign", "description": "Launch campaign for new product line", "status": "active", "owner": "user_def456", "created_at": "2026-03-15T08:30:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-10T14:22:00Z" } ], "pagination": { "page": 1, "per_page": 20, "total": 45, "total_pages": 3 } } ``` #### Create Project ```http POST /projects ``` **Request:** ```json { "name": "Q2 Marketing Campaign", "description": "Launch campaign for new product line", "owner_id": "user_def456", "settings": { "visibility": "private", "default_task_status": "backlog" } } ``` #### Get Project ```http GET /projects/{project_id} ``` #### Update Project ```http PATCH /projects/{project_id} ``` **Request:** ```json { "name": "Updated Project Name", "status": "archived" } ``` #### Delete Project ```http DELETE /projects/{project_id} ``` --- ### Tasks #### List Tasks ```http GET /projects/{project_id}/tasks?status=in_progress&assignee=me ``` **Query Parameters:** | Parameter | Type | Description | |-----------|------|-------------| | `status` | string | Filter by status | | `assignee` | string | Filter by assignee (use `me` for current user) | | `priority` | string | Filter by priority | | `due_before` | ISO 8601 | Filter by due date | #### Create Task ```http POST /projects/{project_id}/tasks ``` **Request:** ```json { "title": "Design landing page mockups", "description": "Create initial designs for the campaign landing page", "assignee_id": "user_ghi789", "priority": "high", "status": "todo", "due_date": "2026-04-20T17:00:00Z", "tags": ["design", "frontend"] } ``` **Response:** ```json { "id": "task_jkl012", "title": "Design landing page mockups", "description": "Create initial designs for the campaign landing page", "assignee": { "id": "user_ghi789", "name": "Sarah Chen", "email": "sarah.chen@example.com" }, "priority": "high", "status": "todo", "due_date": "2026-04-20T17:00:00Z", "tags": ["design", "frontend"], "created_at": "2026-04-13T09:15:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-13T09:15:00Z" } ``` #### Update Task ```http PATCH /tasks/{task_id} ``` **Request:** ```json { "status": "in_progress", "assignee_id": "user_mno345" } ``` --- ### Notifications #### List Notifications ```http GET /notifications?unread_only=true&limit=10 ``` **Response:** ```json { "data": [ { "id": "not_pqr678", "type": "task_assigned", "title": "New task assigned", "body": "You have been assigned to 'Design landing page mockups'", "metadata": { "task_id": "task_jkl012", "project_id": "proj_abc123" }, "is_read": false, "created_at": "2026-04-13T08:30:00Z" } ], "unread_count": 5 } ``` #### Mark as Read ```http PATCH /notifications/{notification_id}/read ``` #### Mark All as Read ```http PATCH /notifications/mark-all-read ``` --- ### Search #### Global Search ```http GET /search?q=landing%20page&entity_type=tasks&page=1 ``` **Query Parameters:** | Parameter | Type | Description | |-----------|------|-------------| | `q` | string | Search query | | `entity_type` | string | Filter by entity (tasks, projects, users) | | `project_id` | string | Scope search to specific project | | `filters` | JSON | Advanced filters (URL-encoded) | **Response:** ```json { "data": [ { "type": "task", "id": "task_jkl012", "title": "Design landing page mockups", "highlight": "Create initial designs for the landing page", "score": 0.95 } ], "facets": { "status": { "todo": 5, "in_progress": 3, "done": 2 }, "priority": { "high": 4, "medium": 4, "low": 2 } }, "pagination": { "page": 1, "per_page": 20, "total": 10 } } ``` #### Autocomplete Suggestions ```http GET /search/suggestions?q=land&limit=5 ``` --- ## Error Handling ### HTTP Status Codes | Status | Description | |--------|-------------| | 200 | OK - Request successful | | 201 | Created - Resource created successfully | | 204 | No Content - Request successful, no body | | 400 | Bad Request - Invalid request format | | 401 | Unauthorized - Invalid or missing authentication | | 403 | Forbidden - Insufficient permissions | | 404 | Not Found - Resource does not exist | | 409 | Conflict - Resource conflict (e.g., duplicate) | | 422 | Unprocessable Entity - Validation error | | 429 | Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded | | 500 | Internal Server Error - Server error | | 503 | Service Unavailable - Temporary service outage | ### Error Response Format All errors follow a consistent format: ```json { "error": { "code": "validation_error", "message": "The request failed validation", "details": [ { "field": "email", "message": "Invalid email format" }, { "field": "name", "message": "Name is required" } ], "request_id": "req_stu901", "timestamp": "2026-04-13T09:15:00Z" } } ``` ### Error Codes | Code | Description | Resolution | |------|-------------|------------| | `invalid_token` | Authentication token is invalid or expired | Refresh your access token | | `insufficient_scope` | Token lacks required permissions | Request additional scopes | | `resource_not_found` | Requested resource does not exist | Verify resource ID | | `validation_error` | Request failed validation | Check request format | | `rate_limited` | Too many requests | Implement exponential backoff | | `conflict` | Resource conflict occurred | Resolve conflict and retry | ### Error Handling Best Practices #### 1. Always Check Status Codes ```python import requests response = requests.get('https://api.oriontech.com/v2/projects') if response.status_code == 200: data = response.json() elif response.status_code == 401: # Token expired, refresh and retry refresh_token() response = retry_request() elif response.status_code == 429: # Rate limited, implement backoff time.sleep(60) response = retry_request() else: error = response.json() log.error(f"API Error: {error['error']['message']}") ``` #### 2. Implement Exponential Backoff ```python import time import random def exponential_backoff(attempt): """Calculate delay with exponential backoff and jitter.""" base_delay = 2 ** attempt jitter = random.uniform(0, 1) return min(base_delay + jitter, 60) # Cap at 60 seconds # Usage max_retries = 3 for attempt in range(max_retries): response = make_request() if response.status_code != 429: break if attempt < max_retries - 1: delay = exponential_backoff(attempt) time.sleep(delay) ``` #### 3. Handle Network Errors ```python from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter from requests.packages.urllib3.util.retry import Retry # Configure retry strategy retry_strategy = Retry( total=3, backoff_factor=1, status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504], ) adapter = HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry_strategy) http = requests.Session() http.mount("https://", adapter) http.mount("http://", adapter) ``` #### 4. Log Request IDs Always log the `request_id` from error responses for debugging: ```python except OrionAPIError as e: logger.error( f"API request failed", extra={ "request_id": e.request_id, "error_code": e.code, "status_code": e.status_code } ) ``` --- ## Rate Limiting Orion API implements rate limiting to ensure fair usage. ### Limits | Endpoint Category | Limit | Window | |-------------------|-------|--------| | Standard API | 1000 | Per minute | | Search | 100 | Per minute | | Authentication | 10 | Per minute | | Export | 10 | Per hour | ### Rate Limit Headers ```http X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000 X-RateLimit-Remaining: 999 X-RateLimit-Reset: 1712973600 X-RateLimit-Policy: 1000;w=60 ``` ### Handling Rate Limits When you receive a `429` response: 1. Check `X-RateLimit-Reset` header 2. Wait until the reset timestamp 3. Resume requests --- ## Webhooks Configure webhooks to receive real-time event notifications. ### Configuration ```http POST /webhooks ``` **Request:** ```json { "url": "https://your-app.com/webhooks/orion", "events": ["task.created", "task.updated", "project.archived"], "secret": "your_webhook_secret" } ``` ### Webhook Payload ```json { "event": "task.created", "timestamp": "2026-04-13T09:15:00Z", "data": { "id": "task_jkl012", "title": "Design landing page mockups", "project_id": "proj_abc123", "created_by": "user_def456" } } ``` ### Security Verify webhook signatures using your secret: ```python import hmac import hashlib def verify_webhook(payload, signature, secret): expected = hmac.new( secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256 ).hexdigest() return hmac.compare_digest(f"sha256={expected}", signature) ``` --- ## SDKs & Tools ### Official SDKs - [Python SDK](https://github.com/oriontech/python-sdk) - [JavaScript/Node.js SDK](https://github.com/oriontech/js-sdk) - [Go SDK](https://github.com/oriontech/go-sdk) ### Postman Collection Download our [Postman Collection](https://docs.oriontech.com/postman) for easy API exploration. ### OpenAPI Specification ``` https://api.oriontech.com/v2/openapi.json ``` --- ## Support - **Documentation:** https://docs.oriontech.com - **Support Email:** api-support@oriontech.com - **Status Page:** https://status.oriontech.com - **Developer Forum:** https://community.oriontech.com --- ## Changelog ### v2.3.0 (2026-04-13) - Added real-time notifications endpoints - Added advanced search API - Added RBAC permission system - Improved error messages with request IDs ### v2.2.0 (2026-03-01) - Added webhook support - Added batch operations for tasks - Improved rate limiting headers
